question: In biased agonism, what distinguishes a beta-arrestin-biased agonist from a G protein-biased agonist at a GPCR?
options: Beta-arrestin-biased agonists activate only Gq signaling
G protein-biased agonists preferentially activate G protein pathways over beta-arrestin recruitment
Beta-arrestin-biased agonists have no signaling activity
G protein-biased agonists cannot bind the receptor
explanation: Biased agonists selectively activate specific downstream pathways. G protein-biased agonists preferentially engage G protein signaling while minimizing beta-arrestin recruitment, potentially reducing side effects.
